desc: Havana blends grand colonial architecture, classic-car boulevards, and a deeply musical street culture, with the driest and most comfortable conditions from late winter into spring.
hls: UNESCO-listed Old Havana plazas, fortifications, and pastel colonial streets
Strong live-music scene spanning son, salsa, jazz clubs, and neighborhood bars
El Malecón waterfront for sunset walks and local social life
Rich museum and revolutionary-history stops across Centro and Vedado
Easy day-trip options to beaches and tobacco-country landscapes
Best comfort window is generally December to April before wetter heat builds

todo.name: Explore Habana Vieja on foot
todo.desc: Walk Plaza Vieja, Plaza de Armas, and Catedral zones early to enjoy cooler streets and lighter tour traffic.

todo.name: Sunset on the Malecón
todo.desc: Join locals on the seawall at golden hour for classic city views and relaxed evening atmosphere.

todo.name: Ride in a classic car through Vedado
todo.desc: Take a short pre-booked route along Paseo and the waterfront to orient neighborhoods quickly.

todo.name: Visit Museo Nacional de Bellas Artes
todo.desc: Split time between Cuban and international collections for a compact overview of national art history.

todo.name: Live music night in Centro Habana
todo.desc: Book a small venue with seated sets if you want quality performance without large club crowds.

todo.name: Day trip to Viñales or Playas del Este
todo.desc: Choose Viñales for tobacco-valley scenery or east-coast beaches for an easier low-logistics day out.

wifi.note: Higher-end hotels and some coworking/cafés are workable, but speeds and uptime are inconsistent citywide.

prac.fltNote: No routine nonstop UK-Havana schedule; fares spike around Christmas/New Year and UK school-holiday windows, so book long-haul + regional legs early.
prac.currency: Cuban Peso (CUP); card acceptance is uneven, so carry backup cash for smaller venues.
prac.tz: CST (UTC-5), daylight saving may apply seasonally.
prac.visa: Most visitors need a tourist card/e-visa equivalent and onward proof; always verify current requirements for your passport.
alerts: Summer and early autumn are hotter, wetter, and more cyclone-exposed than winter/spring months.
Mobile data and ATMs can be patchy; arrange key bookings and cash strategy before arrival.
Museum closures and transport frequency can vary on public holidays and Sundays.

sweet: December to April for the best balance of lower rain, manageable heat, and strong city-walk conditions.
